description_long: This is a rectangular, black radio receiver with a robust metal chassis and a utilitarian design, commonly found in vehicles or as a desktop communication device. The front panel features a digital red LED display, several rotary knobs, push buttons, and labeled switches for band selection, channel, volume, and squelch control. The top surface has a grid of ventilation holes and white printed operational instructions. The rear panel includes a power input labeled for DC 11-16V, a fuse holder, and a ground terminal, with visible wear indicating frequent handling. The sides are plain, with subtle screw heads securing the casing. The overall appearance is functional and industrial, suitable for urban utility or emergency communication scenarios.
description: A black, rectangular radio receiver with a digital display, multiple control knobs, and a metal chassis, designed for robust urban or vehicular use. The front panel is detailed with controls and a red LED screen, while the rear features power and ground connections.
description_view_0: The left side of the radio receiver, showing a plain black metal surface with subtle screw heads and no visible controls or ports.
description_view_1: The rear panel, displaying a power input labeled DC 11-16V, a fuse holder, a ground terminal, and some operational instructions printed in white.
description_view_2: The right side, similar to the left, is a plain black surface with visible screws and no interactive features.
description_view_3: The front panel, featuring a red LED display, multiple rotary knobs, push buttons, and labeled controls for band, channel, volume, and squelch.
